'''Aaron Pene''' (born 26 September 1995) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

==Background Early life==
Pene is of German and Samoan heritage.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/03/23/league-of-nations-stars-representing-40-countries/ |title=League of nations:Stars representing 40 countries |publisher=NRL.com |first=Brad |last=Walter |date=23 March 2022}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/rugby-league-warriors-sign-late-bloomer-aaron-pene-from-melbourne-storm/OV3SCC4D7QGRR43XCFZT4HF3W4/ |title=Warriors sign 'late bloomer' Aaron Pene from Melbourne Storm |publisher=[[The New Zealand Herald]] |first=Michael |last=Burgess |date=8 June 2021}}</ref> Pene played his junior rugby league for [[Berala Bears]] and [[Chester Hill Hornets]], before being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref name="StormBio">{{cite web |last1=Lulham |first1=Amanda |title=Aaron Pene: Determined to succeed |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2020/04/28/aaron-pene-determined-to-succeed/ |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=25 July 2022 |date=28 April 2020}}</ref>

He played in the [[NRL Under-20s]] for [[Parramatta Eels]] in 2015, and [[Wentworthville Magpies]] in 2017. He injured his knee at the end of 2017 after signing a trial contract with [[Newtown Jets]].<ref name="StormBio"/>

==Career==
===Melbourne Storm: 2019-2021===
While playing for the [[Central Queensland Capras]] in the [[2019 Queensland Cup]] competition, Pene signed with [[Melbourne Storm]] in June 2019, moving to play for Storm feeder club [[Brisbane Tigers]].<ref name="StormBio"/>
Promoted to the club's top 30 for the 2020 season, Pene made his debut in round 20 of the [[2020 NRL season]] for the Melbourne Storm against [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]], in a 30–22 loss at [[Jubilee Oval|Netstrata Jubilee Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/22/round-20-nrl-squad-announcements/ |title=NRL Round 20 team lists |publisher=NRL.com |date=22 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |title=Aaron Pene: This is just the beginning |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2020/10/01/aaron-pene-this-is-just-the-beginning/ |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=25 July 2022 |date=1 October 2020}}</ref>

===New Zealand Warriors: 2022===
On 8 June 2021, the [[New Zealand Warriors]] announced that Pene would be joining them on a two-year contract starting 2022.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Becht |first1=Richard |title=Storm prop Pene signed |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2021/06/08/storm-prop-pene-signed/ |website=NZ Warriors |date=8 June 2021 |access-date=8 June 2021}}</ref> Pene would appear in 14 matches for the Warriors, also making appearances for [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] competition. He was granted an early release from his contract by the club in October.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Becht |first1=Richard |title=Vodafone Warriors grant Pene early release from contract |url=https://www.warriors.kiwi/news/2022/10/04/vodafone-warriors-grant-pene-early-release-from-contract/ |website=warriors.kiwi |publisher=[[New Zealand Warriors]] |access-date=6 October 2022 |date=4 October 2022}}</ref>

===Melbourne Storm: 2023–2024===
Pene rejoined the Melbourne Storm in October 2022, signing a two-year contract to return to where he made his NRL debut in 2020.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Gabor |first1=Martin |title=Storm continues signing spree by landing Warriors big man |url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/storm-continues-signing-spree-by-landing-warriors-big-man/news-story/e18dc3dc4403638782f2fc262065d996 |website=news.com.au |publisher=News Corporation Australia |access-date=11 October 2022 |date=12 October 2022}}</ref>
Pene only played eight matches for Melbourne in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished third on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-melbourne-storm-season-review/af9dab1e4f900cda437dc53f#:~:text=The%20expectations%20for%20the%20Storm,clubs%2C%20but%20not%20the%20Storm./|title=NRL 2023: Melbourne Storm season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

He was released from his contract with Melbourne during the [[2024 NRL season]] to take up an opportunity in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Pene Granted Release to Super League |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2024/05/24/pene-granted-release-to-super-league/ |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=24 May 2024 |date=24 May 2024}}</ref>
